channel.title: Light Tower Group — NYC CRE Capital Markets Insights
channel.description: Data-driven capital markets analysis and building intelligence for NYC commercial real estate professionals.

item.title: Midtown East Adaptive Reuse: Four Projects Signal Shift in Office-to-Residential Economics
item.description: TF Cornerstone, Vanbarton, Rudin, and NYC EDC advance four Midtown East projects totaling 1.4M SF. Office-to-residential conversions under 467-m and M-CORE programs signal shifting underwriting.

news:news.news:title: Midtown East Adaptive Reuse: Four Projects Signal Shift in Office-to-Residential Economics
news:news.news:keywords: adaptive reuse, office-to-residential, Midtown East, TF Cornerstone, Vanbarton Group, Rudin Management, M-CORE, 467-m

item.title: Two Trees' Refinery at Domino Hits 90% Occupancy: A Bet on Brooklyn Office Pays Off
item.description: Two Trees' Refinery at Domino in Brooklyn hits 90% occupancy, leasing 450K sf to tech and media tenants. The $150M conversion of a 167-year-old sugar plant signals shifting office demand away from Manhattan.

news:news.news:title: Two Trees' Refinery at Domino Hits 90% Occupancy: A Bet on Brooklyn Office Pays Off
news:news.news:keywords: Two Trees, Refinery at Domino, Brooklyn office, Williamsburg, office occupancy

item.title: The $145 Trillion Bond Market Ends the Free Lunch for Sovereign Borrowers
item.description: The $145 trillion global bond market signals higher borrowing costs for governments as supply disruptions and massive debt needs collide. The era of free fiscal lunch is over.

news:news.news:title: The $145 Trillion Bond Market Ends the Free Lunch for Sovereign Borrowers
news:news.news:keywords: bond market, sovereign debt, inflation, fiscal policy, borrowing costs

item.title: AvalonBay-Equity Residential $69B Merger: Scale as Survival in Apartment REITs
item.description: AvalonBay and Equity Residential merge in a $69B deal to form the largest US apartment owner. Cost cuts of $175M targeted as 480,000 new units hit the market and pricing power evaporates.

news:news.news:title: AvalonBay-Equity Residential $69B Merger: Scale as Survival in Apartment REITs
news:news.news:keywords: AvalonBay, Equity Residential, REIT consolidation, apartment oversupply, multifamily M&A

item.title: Treasury Curve Flashes Higher-for-Longer Warning Under Warsh
item.description: Key Treasury yield gap tightens to one-year low as markets bet on higher-for-longer rates under Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. Implications for CRE debt costs and cap rates.

news:news.news:title: Treasury Curve Flashes Higher-for-Longer Warning Under Warsh
news:news.news:keywords: Treasury yield curve, Kevin Warsh, Federal Reserve, higher-for-longer, CRE debt costs

item.title: Public-Private Partnerships: The Only Fix for a Structural Housing Crisis
item.description: Inflation and structural supply gaps push public-private partnerships to the forefront of U.S. housing policy. Developers, investors, and government must align capital and regulation to address the affordability crisis.

news:news.news:title: Public-Private Partnerships: The Only Fix for a Structural Housing Crisis
news:news.news:keywords: public-private partnerships, housing crisis, inflation, construction costs, affordable housing

item.title: House THUD Bill Cuts Public Housing $1.25B, Vouchers Flat at $34B
item.description: House THUD bill provides $34B for Section 8 vouchers but cuts public housing by $1.25B. CDBG and HOME survive elimination. Advocates call for $77.3B HUD funding.

news:news.news:title: House THUD Bill Cuts Public Housing $1.25B, Vouchers Flat at $34B
news:news.news:keywords: THUD, HUD, Section 8, public housing, CDBG

item.title: Willets Point Phase One Delivers 880 Affordable Units as NYC Bets Big on Public-Private Scale
item.description: Related Companies and Sterling Equities deliver 880 affordable units at Willets Point Commons in Queens. Phase one of a 2,500-unit master plan backed by HPD, HDC, and NYCEDC.

news:news.news:title: Willets Point Phase One Delivers 880 Affordable Units as NYC Bets Big on Public-Private Scale
news:news.news:keywords: Willets Point, Related Companies, Sterling Equities, affordable housing, Queens development

item.title: BlackRock’s Saigal Sees Fed Cut Justification as Warsh Era Begins
item.description: BlackRock's Rohan Saigal says 'sufficient factors' justify a Fed rate cut under new chair Kevin Warsh, signaling a shift in institutional rate expectations.

news:news.news:title: BlackRock’s Saigal Sees Fed Cut Justification as Warsh Era Begins
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, BlackRock, Kevin Warsh, interest rates, monetary policy

item.title: Global Capital Returns to U.S. CRE, But With a Sharper Edge
item.description: McCourt Partners president Jordan Lang details renewed global capital interest in U.S. CRE, with focus on industrial, multifamily, and structured partnerships amid improved macro stability.

news:news.news:title: Global Capital Returns to U.S. CRE, But With a Sharper Edge
news:news.news:keywords: global capital, McCourt Partners, Jordan Lang, industrial, multifamily, capital flows

item.title: Richard Born Exits Chambers Hotel at $860K Per Key as Sonder Collapse Reshapes Midtown
item.description: Richard Born's BD Hotels sells Chambers Hotel at 15 West 56th Street for $66.2M to Hennick Group. The $860K per key deal follows Sonder's collapse and a decade of restaurant turnover.

news:news.news:title: Richard Born Exits Chambers Hotel at $860K Per Key as Sonder Collapse Reshapes Midtown
news:news.news:keywords: Richard Born, BD Hotels, Chambers Hotel, Sonder, Hennick Group, Midtown Manhattan, hotel sales

item.title: RealPage Rent-Fixing Settlements Top $200 Million as Landlords Pay for Algorithmic Collusion
item.description: RealPage rent-fixing settlements surpass $200M as 11 landlords, including Equity Residential and Camden, agree to stop using nonpublic rental data. DOJ oversight looms.

news:news.news:title: RealPage Rent-Fixing Settlements Top $200 Million as Landlords Pay for Algorithmic Collusion
news:news.news:keywords: RealPage, rent-fixing, multifamily, antitrust, DOJ

item.title: Saunders & Premier Estate: Ultraluxury Brokerages Bridge Hamptons-Palm Beach Gap
item.description: Saunders & Associates and Premier Estate Properties form cross-market referral alliance. The $1.4B Hamptons firm links with Palm Beach's top agents as industry consolidation pressures independents.

news:news.news:title: Saunders & Premier Estate: Ultraluxury Brokerages Bridge Hamptons-Palm Beach Gap
news:news.news:keywords: luxury real estate, brokerage consolidation, Hamptons, Palm Beach, referral partnership

item.title: Mamdani's First Rezoning: Brooklyn Corridors Face Density Bet
item.description: Mayor Mamdani proposes 'South of Prospect Plan' rezoning Coney Island and McDonald avenues for thousands of housing units, aligning with IBX light rail and 200K affordable pledge.

news:news.news:title: Mamdani's First Rezoning: Brooklyn Corridors Face Density Bet
news:news.news:keywords: Zohran Mamdani, Brooklyn rezoning, transit-oriented development, affordable housing, Interborough Express

item.title: J-51 Reboot: Albany Gives Co-ops a Win, Rent-Regulated Landlords a Zero
item.description: NY's J-51 reboot extends tax break to 2036, raises co-op/condo threshold to $60K, but keeps 50% affordability rule, freezing out rent-regulated landlords.

news:news.news:title: J-51 Reboot: Albany Gives Co-ops a Win, Rent-Regulated Landlords a Zero
news:news.news:keywords: J-51, New York City, rent regulation, co-ops, condos, property tax

item.title: Benchmark Buys Heller's Upper West Side Asset at 40% Discount to Prior Deal
item.description: Benchmark Real Estate Group buys 698 West End Avenue from Heller Organization for $42M, a 36% discount per unit vs. prior deal. Heller pivots to luxury as Benchmark consolidates prewar portfolio.

news:news.news:title: Benchmark Buys Heller's Upper West Side Asset at 40% Discount to Prior Deal
news:news.news:keywords: Benchmark Real Estate Group, Heller Organization, Upper West Side, New York City multifamily, distressed sales

item.title: Mamdani Targets Brooklyn Transit Corridor for First Neighborhood Rezoning
item.description: Mayor Mamdani launches South of Prospect Plan to rezone Brooklyn blocks near future IBX light rail, targeting thousands of new housing units amid NYC's housing crisis.

news:news.news:title: Mamdani Targets Brooklyn Transit Corridor for First Neighborhood Rezoning
news:news.news:keywords: Mamdani, Brooklyn rezoning, IBX, transit-oriented development, housing policy

item.title: New York's Pied-à-Terre Tax: A $200,000 Surcharge That Rewrites Luxury CRE Math
item.description: New York's pied-à-terre tax could add $200,000 annually to an $18M co-op. The sales-based surcharge triples carrying costs, pushing buyers to rent and reshaping luxury CRE demand.

news:news.news:title: New York's Pied-à-Terre Tax: A $200,000 Surcharge That Rewrites Luxury CRE Math
news:news.news:keywords: pied-a-terre tax, New York real estate, luxury housing, Kathy Hochul, cash buyer tax, co-op market, rental demand, property tax

item.title: STV-Turner JV to Manage $10B NYC Bus Terminal: A Test of Public Infrastructure Delivery
item.description: STV-Turner JV named construction manager for Phase 1 of the $10B Midtown Bus Terminal replacement. The 900,000-sq-ft interim terminal and ramp work, with Tutor Perini as contractor, tests public infrastructure delivery in Manhattan.

news:news.news:title: STV-Turner JV to Manage $10B NYC Bus Terminal: A Test of Public Infrastructure Delivery
news:news.news:keywords: STV, Turner Construction, Port Authority, Midtown Bus Terminal, infrastructure, mega-project

item.title: Carmel Partners Buys 49% of UWS Portfolio at $485M, Steps Into 2.6% Fannie Mae Debt
item.description: Carmel Partners closes on 49% stake in five-building Upper West Side portfolio valued at $485M. In-place Fannie Mae debt at 2.6% through 2031. UDR remains majority owner.

news:news.news:title: Carmel Partners Buys 49% of UWS Portfolio at $485M, Steps Into 2.6% Fannie Mae Debt
news:news.news:keywords: Carmel Partners, UDR, MetLife, Upper West Side, multifamily, Fannie Mae, Newmark, Columbus Square Collection

item.title: The Fixed-Rate Mortgage That Isn't: Escrow Shortages Hit 65% of Borrowers
item.description: 65% of escrow accounts face shortages averaging $2,157 as property taxes and insurance surge. Florida and Colorado see 70%+ jumps. Fixed-rate mortgages no longer fix housing costs.

news:news.news:title: The Fixed-Rate Mortgage That Isn't: Escrow Shortages Hit 65% of Borrowers
news:news.news:keywords: escrow, mortgage, property taxes, homeowners insurance, housing costs

item.title: Bert Dweck's $14.4M Legal Avalanche: Brooklyn's Trust Crisis
item.description: Bert Dweck faces seven lawsuits seeking $14.4M over unpaid loans, bad checks, and diverted escrow. The pattern reveals how Brooklyn's trust-based lending is cracking under rate pressure.

news:news.news:title: Bert Dweck's $14.4M Legal Avalanche: Brooklyn's Trust Crisis
news:news.news:keywords: Bert Dweck, Dweck Group, Brooklyn real estate, litigation, private lending

item.title: Institutional Capital Flies Over the Coasts: Oklahoma's $1.2B Pension Play
item.description: Oklahoma Commerce CEO John Budd details how institutional investors are allocating $1.2B to interior markets, driven by demographic growth, energy capacity, and cap rate spreads of 200+ bps over coastal gateways.

news:news.news:title: Institutional Capital Flies Over the Coasts: Oklahoma's $1.2B Pension Play
news:news.news:keywords: institutional capital, Oklahoma, flyover markets, pension funds, cap rate compression

item.title: Connecticut Pension Commits $200M to Stockbridge's Smart Markets Fund
item.description: Connecticut Retirement Plans and Trust Fund commits $200M to Stockbridge Smart Markets Fund, an open-end vehicle targeting 15 high-growth U.S. markets. Louisiana Teachers committed $400M in 2024.

news:news.news:title: Connecticut Pension Commits $200M to Stockbridge's Smart Markets Fund
news:news.news:keywords: Connecticut Retirement Plans and Trust Fund, Stockbridge Capital Group, Smart Markets Fund, pension fund real estate, open-end fund

item.title: MFS Collapse Exposes $1.3B Hole, Banks and Credit Giants on the Hook
item.description: MFS collapse triggers $1.3B shortfall, exposing Barclays, HSBC, Jefferies, Wells Fargo, Apollo, and Elliott to potential losses. Double-pledging allegations raise systemic risk.

news:news.news:title: MFS Collapse Exposes $1.3B Hole, Banks and Credit Giants on the Hook
news:news.news:keywords: MFS, private credit, bridge lending, double pledging, systemic risk

item.title: NYC Pied-à-Terre Tax: Two Years of Fuzzy Math and Market Uncertainty
item.description: NY Gov. Hochul's pied-à-terre tax proposal uses a flawed 'market value' metric, delaying implementation by two years. Luxury condo investors face valuation uncertainty.

news:news.news:title: NYC Pied-à-Terre Tax: Two Years of Fuzzy Math and Market Uncertainty
news:news.news:keywords: pied-à-terre tax, New York City, Kathy Hochul, luxury condos, valuation, policy, market uncertainty

item.title: Extell Pays $451M for Park Avenue Site as Corem Exits NYC at a Discount
item.description: Gary Barnett's Extell pays $451M for 405-407 Park Avenue, below the $500M ask. Swedish seller Corem Property Group exits NYC at a loss, signaling shifting institutional appetite for Manhattan development.

news:news.news:title: Extell Pays $451M for Park Avenue Site as Corem Exits NYC at a Discount
news:news.news:keywords: Extell, Corem Property Group, Park Avenue, New York City development, institutional capital

item.title: Mortgage Rates Hit Yearly Highs, Yet Housing Demand Holds: A Fragile Equilibrium
item.description: Pending home sales rose to 78,006 and purchase apps up 7% YoY despite mortgage rates at yearly highs. But the Iran conflict and dwindling oil reserves threaten a summer demand collapse.

news:news.news:title: Mortgage Rates Hit Yearly Highs, Yet Housing Demand Holds: A Fragile Equilibrium
news:news.news:keywords: mortgage rates, housing demand, pending sales, purchase applications, Iran conflict

item.title: NYC Cash-Sale Tax Plan Threatens 75% of Million-Dollar Deals
item.description: NYC legislators propose 1% tax on cash home sales over $1M, targeting 75% of luxury deals. Industry warns of market chill, revenue shortfall, and buyer flight.

news:news.news:title: NYC Cash-Sale Tax Plan Threatens 75% of Million-Dollar Deals
news:news.news:keywords: New York City, cash sales, real estate tax, pied-à-terre tax, residential market

item.title: Vornado Files for 1,090-Foot Tribeca Supertall, 976 Units, 2032 Target
item.description: Vornado Realty Trust and Stellar Management file EAS for 1,090-foot, 976-unit supertall at 310 Greenwich Street. Project bypasses ULURP, targets 2032 completion. 251 affordable units under UAP.

news:news.news:title: Vornado Files for 1,090-Foot Tribeca Supertall, 976 Units, 2032 Target
news:news.news:keywords: Vornado Realty Trust, Stellar Management, 310 Greenwich Street, Tribeca, supertall, affordable housing

item.title: Dermot Lands $355M Refinancing for Upper West Side Tower as Multifamily Debt Markets Thaw
item.description: Dermot Company secures $355M refinancing for a 2016-built Upper West Side rental tower. The deal signals lender appetite for stabilized urban multifamily assets amid a thawing debt market.

news:news.news:title: Dermot Lands $355M Refinancing for Upper West Side Tower as Multifamily Debt Markets Thaw
news:news.news:keywords: Dermot Company, Upper West Side, multifamily refinancing, New York City, debt markets

item.title: Manhattan Rents Hit $5,099 Median as Policy Chokehold Tightens Supply
item.description: Manhattan median rent hits $5,099 in April 2026, up 6% YoY. Vacancy at 1.55%, inventory down 25%. Corcoran data shows policy-driven supply crunch, not demand surge.

news:news.news:title: Manhattan Rents Hit $5,099 Median as Policy Chokehold Tightens Supply
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan rents, rental market, Corcoran, vacancy rate, housing policy

item.title: Inflation Forecast Nearly Doubles to 6%, Pressuring Mortgage Rates and CRE Debt Markets
item.description: Survey of Professional Forecasters projects CPI inflation at 6% for Q2 2026, up from 2.7% three months ago. Energy price surge, Fed transition, and implications for CRE debt costs.

news:news.news:title: Inflation Forecast Nearly Doubles to 6%, Pressuring Mortgage Rates and CRE Debt Markets
news:news.news:keywords: inflation, mortgage rates, Federal Reserve, Survey of Professional Forecasters, CRE debt

item.title: Bond Yields Hit Year High as New Fed Chair Warsh Faces Stubborn Inflation
item.description: 30-year Treasury yield hits 5.114%, highest since May 2025, as CPI rises 3.8% and PPI surges 6%. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh inherits a deteriorating inflation picture with mortgage rates approaching 7%.

news:news.news:title: Bond Yields Hit Year High as New Fed Chair Warsh Faces Stubborn Inflation
news:news.news:keywords: Treasury yields, mortgage rates, Kevin Warsh, inflation, Federal Reserve

item.title: 20 Times Square Loan Returns to Special Servicing as Retail Vacancy Persists
item.description: Maefield Development's $647.5M CMBS loan on 20 Times Square returns to special servicing after missing May 2026 maturity. Retail vacancy and NFL departure weigh on ground lease revenue.

news:news.news:title: 20 Times Square Loan Returns to Special Servicing as Retail Vacancy Persists
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, special servicing, Maefield Development, 20 Times Square, retail vacancy

item.title: Charles Cohen's D&D Building Default: A Pattern of Distress at 979 Third Avenue
item.description: Charles Cohen's D&D Building at 979 Third Avenue defaults on $150M CMBS loan, transferred to special servicing. Occupancy fell to 63%, revenue down 24% in a decade. Ground lease escalations compound distress.

news:news.news:title: Charles Cohen's D&D Building Default: A Pattern of Distress at 979 Third Avenue
news:news.news:keywords: Charles Cohen, 979 Third Avenue, D&D Building, CMBS default, special servicing, Green Loan Services, Cohen Brothers Realty, ground lease, Fortress Credit, foreclosure

item.title: Vornado, Stellar Bet 976 Units on Tribeca Density Play as City Scrambles for Housing
item.description: Vornado Realty Trust and Stellar Management file plans for 976-unit residential tower at Independence Plaza in Tribeca, using NYC's Universal Affordability Preference program to unlock density.

news:news.news:title: Vornado, Stellar Bet 976 Units on Tribeca Density Play as City Scrambles for Housing
news:news.news:keywords: Vornado Realty Trust, Stellar Management, Independence Plaza, Tribeca, Universal Affordability Preference, multifamily development, New York City housing

item.title: CMBS Reappraisals Hit 53% Median Discount in 2025, Urban Office Leads Losses
item.description: Trepp data shows 2025 CMBS reappraisals at 53% median discount; urban office at -64% and 2017-2018 vintage at -71% signal structural impairment, not cyclical dip.

news:news.news:title: CMBS Reappraisals Hit 53% Median Discount in 2025, Urban Office Leads Losses
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, reappraisals, office, valuation, distressed CRE

item.title: Vornado, Stellar File Plans for 72-Story Tribeca Tower as City of Yes Clears Path
item.description: Vornado and Stellar file plans for a 72-story, 976-unit tower at Independence Plaza, bypassing ULURP. The $675M CMBS refinancing and $33M in upgrades signal a new phase for the Tribeca complex.

news:news.news:title: Vornado, Stellar File Plans for 72-Story Tribeca Tower as City of Yes Clears Path
news:news.news:keywords: Vornado Realty Trust, Stellar Management, Independence Plaza, Tribeca, City of Yes, CMBS, affordable housing, ULURP

item.title: Vornado-Aurora JV Refis Chelsea Office at Wider Spreads, Longer Maturity
item.description: Vornado and Aurora Capital refinance 61 Ninth Avenue with a $161M UBS loan at SOFR+300bps, replacing a $155M loan at SOFR+245bps. The 194K SF Chelsea office building is 100% leased to Aetna and Starbucks.

news:news.news:title: Vornado-Aurora JV Refis Chelsea Office at Wider Spreads, Longer Maturity
news:news.news:keywords: Vornado Realty Trust, Aurora Capital Associates, UBS, 61 Ninth Avenue, office refinancing, Chelsea, Manhattan, interest-only loan, SOFR, Walker & Dunlop

item.title: The Day the Music Stopped
item.description: A new Fed chair, a 6% inflation bombshell, and the reckoning that American commercial real estate can no longer defer.

news:news.news:title: The Day the Music Stopped
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, Kevin Warsh, inflation, PPI, commercial real estate, CRE debt, CMBS, maturity wall

item.title: Bond Yields Hit 2026 High as PPI Surge Crushes Rate-Cut Hopes
item.description: April PPI surges 1.4% monthly, 6% annual—double forecasts. 10-year Treasury hits 4.49%, 30-year above 5%. Mortgage rates set to climb further as Fed rate-cut expectations evaporate.

news:news.news:title: Bond Yields Hit 2026 High as PPI Surge Crushes Rate-Cut Hopes
news:news.news:keywords: Treasury yields, PPI, mortgage rates, Federal Reserve, inflation

item.title: Spring Market Stalls as 30-Year Fixed Hits 6.57% on Hot Inflation Data
item.description: 30-year fixed mortgage rate jumps to 6.57% after hotter PPI and CPI reports. Buyer power down 4% from February. Spring market recovery stalls as rates reverse March declines.

news:news.news:title: Spring Market Stalls as 30-Year Fixed Hits 6.57% on Hot Inflation Data
news:news.news:keywords: mortgage rates, spring market, inflation, PPI, CPI, buyer power, housing inventory

item.title: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as Fed Chair: CRE Braces for Rate Policy Shift
item.description: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed chair in 54-45 vote. CRE industry expects lower rates and revised bank capital rules. MBA and CREFC signal engagement.

news:news.news:title: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as Fed Chair: CRE Braces for Rate Policy Shift
news:news.news:keywords: Kevin Warsh, Federal Reserve, interest rates, CRE capital requirements, monetary policy

item.title: Brookfield and Qatar Tap $1.9B Refi for Two Manhattan West, Pocket $273M
item.description: Brookfield and QIA secure $1.9B refinancing for Two Manhattan West via Wells Fargo, extracting $273M in cash. The 97%-leased tower at $132/sf gross rent signals a trophy office refinancing wave.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield and Qatar Tap $1.9B Refi for Two Manhattan West, Pocket $273M
news:news.news:keywords: Brookfield, Qatar Investment Authority, Two Manhattan West, refinancing, Wells Fargo

item.title: Brookfield, QIA Secure $1.9B CMBS Loan at 107 bps Over Treasuries as Office Market Fractures
item.description: Brookfield and QIA refinance 2 Manhattan West with a $1.9B CMBS loan at 107 bps over the 10-Year Treasury. The 5.53% coupon and $273M cash return signal a bifurcated office market where only the strongest assets access public debt.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield, QIA Secure $1.9B CMBS Loan at 107 bps Over Treasuries as Office Market Fractures
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, Brookfield, Qatar Investment Authority, Manhattan office, refinancing, Wells Fargo

item.title: Office Loans Drive CMBS Special Servicing Rate to 11.38% in April
item.description: CMBS special servicing rate rose 38 bps to 11.38% in April, driven by office loans. $1.93B in new transfers. Office rate up 93 bps. Multifamily up 33 bps.

news:news.news:title: Office Loans Drive CMBS Special Servicing Rate to 11.38% in April
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, special servicing, office, delinquency, Trepp

item.title: Bond Bears Reload: Rate Hike Bets Return as Inflation Sticks
item.description: Traders renew bearish Treasury bets as oil and inflation push higher, lifting expectations for Fed rate hikes. The shift threatens CRE debt costs and cap rates.

news:news.news:title: Bond Bears Reload: Rate Hike Bets Return as Inflation Sticks
news:news.news:keywords: Treasuries, Federal Reserve, inflation, interest rates, CRE capital markets

item.title: Inflation Hits 3.8%, Mortgage Rate Relief Off the Table as Fed Hike Risk Emerges
item.description: April CPI jumps to 3.8%, highest in three years. 10-year Treasury yields rise. Mortgage rates could hit 7%. Fed rate cuts off the table; hike scenario introduced.

news:news.news:title: Inflation Hits 3.8%, Mortgage Rate Relief Off the Table as Fed Hike Risk Emerges
news:news.news:keywords: inflation, mortgage rates, Federal Reserve, CPI, 10-year Treasury, housing market

item.title: Albany’s $8B Bailout Lets Mamdani Avoid Property Tax Hike, Targets Second Homes
item.description: NYC Mayor Mamdani’s FY2027 budget avoids property tax hike thanks to $8B state aid. Pied-à-terre tax targets second homes over $5M, raising $500M annually. Capital markets watch for impact on luxury real estate.

news:news.news:title: Albany’s $8B Bailout Lets Mamdani Avoid Property Tax Hike, Targets Second Homes
news:news.news:keywords: New York City budget, pied-à-terre tax, Zohran Mamdani, Kathy Hochul, municipal finance
